title: ericameria viscidiflora subsp. lanceolata
intro: Ericameria viscidiflora subsp. lanceolata, also known as the sticky greasewood, is a subspecies of shrub within the sunflower family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its resinous sap, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ericameria viscidiflora subsp. lanceolata include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's resin is often used to create salves, tinctures, and infusions for topical and internal use.
